workflow.name: Build an OpenAI HR assistant for Telegram attendance and salary reports
workflow.description:
## What Problem Does It Solve?
- HR managers waste hours manually logging attendance, calculating work hours, and tracking salary advances in spreadsheets.
- Onboarding new staff often involves messy paperwork or scattered chat messages.
- Salary calculations are prone to errors when manually tallying absence, overtime, and penalties.
- This workflow solves these by:
-- Allowing employees to check in/out and request loans via a simple Telegram chat.
-- Automatically calculating work hours and applying penalties for early departure.
-- Registering new employees through an interactive AI chat.
-- Generating instant financial reports including net salary, deductions, and overtime.

## How to Configure It
- Google Sheets Setup
-- Create a Google Sheet with two tabs: one for "Employee Data" (Columns: ID, Name, Role, Branch, Salary) and one for "Logs" (Attendance history).
-- Connect your Google Sheets OAuth2 credentials in n8n and select this sheet in all related nodes.
- Telegram Setup
-- Create a new bot via BotFather and connect your Telegram credentials.
-- (Optional) Set the "Admin Chat ID" in the Daily Report node to receive absentee lists.
- AI Setup
-- Add your OpenAI API key (used for intent classification and conversational agents).
-- The included prompts are currently in Egyptian Arabic — you can translate them to English or your local language in the "System Message" of the Agent nodes.
- Timezone
-- Adjust the timezone in the "Context" section of the AI Agent nodes to match your company's location (currently set to Africa/Cairo).

## How It Works
- Telegram Trigger catches every message sent to the HR Bot.
- AI Classifier analyzes the text to determine intent:
-- New Employee: Triggers a conversation to collect Name, ID, Salary, and Branch -> Validates data -> Adds to "Employee Data" sheet.
-- Attendance (Check-in): Logs the timestamp immediately.
-- Departure (Check-out): Looks up arrival time, calculates total hours worked. If under 8 hours, it flags "Early Departure" and logs it with a penalty note.
-- Financial Request: Logs salary advances (loans) directly to the sheet.
-- Report Request: An AI Analyst calculates net salary (Basic + Overtime - Absence - Advances) and replies with a detailed breakdown.
- Daily Schedule:
-- Every day at 1:00 PM, the workflow compares today's attendance logs against the full employee list.
-- It generates a list of absentees and sends a summary report to the HR Manager.

## Customization Ideas
- Change the Logic: Edit the JavaScript node to change the "8-hour work day" rule to match your company policy.
- Multi-Platform: Swap the Telegram Trigger for WhatsApp or Slack to match your team's communication tool.
- PDF Payslips: Add a node to generate a PDF payslip based on the AI's financial report and email it to the employee.
- Face Recognition: Integrate an image analysis node if you want employees to send a selfie for attendance verification.
- For more info [Contact Me](https://www.linkedin.com/in/khaledyasser01/)

workflow.name: Manage employee onboarding and attendance using Telegram and OpenAI

parameters.content:
### 🤖 HR Automated System

#### How it works
1. **Input:** Receives Telegram messages from employees.
2. **AI Analysis:** Classifies intent (Attendance, Report, or Registration) using OpenAI.
3. **Execution:** 
   - Logs Check-in/out & calculates penalties for early departure.
   - Registers new employees via chat conversation.
   - Generates salary reports based on attendance history.
4. **Automation:** Runs a daily scheduled job to report absentees.

#### Setup steps
1. **Credentials:** Connect your Telegram Bot, OpenAI, and Google Sheets accounts.
2. **Google Sheet:** Create a sheet with columns: `[Name, ID, Role, Branch, Salary]` and a second tab for `Logs`.
3. **IDs:** Replace `[YOUR_SPREADSHEET_ID]` in all Sheet nodes.
4. **Timezone:** Adjust the `Context` in the AI Agent nodes to your local timezone.

## 1. Input & Intent Classification
Receives messages, maintains chat history, and uses AI to determine if the user is a new employee, recording a transaction, or asking for a report.

## 2. New Employee Onboarding
Handles the conversational flow to register new staff. Collects Name, ID, Salary, etc., validates the JSON data, and saves to the database.

## 3. Transaction Logic
Handles existing employees. Logs Attendance, calculates 'Early Departure' penalties based on work hours, and records Salary Advances.

## 4. Financial Reporting
Fetches employee salary and movement history to calculate net pay (deducting absence/penalties and adding overtime).

## 5. Daily Scheduled Jobs
Runs automatically (e.g., at 1:00 PM) to compare today's logs against the full employee list and report who is absent.

parameters.text:
=Sorry, this code ({{ $('Route_Intent').item.json.identifier }}) is not registered in our system! 🤔

If this is a new employee, reply with "Yes" to add them.
If there's a mistake, please double-check the code and try again.

For assistance, contact KHALED YASSER 📞

options.systemMessage:
==# Role: HR Data Extractor & Router
# Objective: Analyze the user's message to determine the intent (Recording Transaction OR Requesting Report) and extract relevant data strictly as JSON.

# Context:
The exact current date and time in Africa/Cairo is: ={{ $now.setZone('Africa/Cairo').toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss a') }}

# Instructions:
Analyze the user's message (English or Arabic) and output a JSON object with these exact fields:

1. "intent": Determine the user's goal. MUST be one of:
   - "record": If user wants to log attendance, absence, money, etc. (e.g., "حضور 1001", "Advance 500").
   - "report": If user asks for info or summary (e.g., "Report for Fatima", "How many days was Mohamed absent?").

2. "action": 
   - If intent is "record": Extract the action type EXACTLY as one of these Arabic terms to match the database: (حضور, انصراف, غياب, نص يوم, سلف, اضافي, اجازه).
   - If intent is "report": Output "report".

3. "identifier": The Employee ID or Name mentioned.
4. "value": Amount or hours (if applicable), otherwise null.
5. "notes": Any extra details.
6. "timestamp": Current time from Context.

# Output Format:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do NOT wrap in markdown blocks.

# Example 1 (Recording):
User: "Clock-in 1001"
Output: { "intent": "record", "action": "حضور", "identifier": "1001", "value": null, "notes": "", "timestamp": "..." }

# Example 2 (Reporting):
User: "Report for Fatima Rouge"
Output: { "intent": "report", "action": "report", "identifier": "Fatima Rouge", "value": null, "notes": "", "timestamp": "..." }

parameters.text:
=Employee Basic Salary: {{ $('Fetch_Salary').item.json['الراتب الاساسي'] }}
Movements Log: {{ JSON.stringify($input.all().map(item => item.json)) }}

options.systemMessage:
=Role: Professional HR Financial Analyst (XQ Pharma)
Objective: Calculate the Net Salary strictly following the company's rules.

# Input Data:
1. Basic Salary.
2. Movement Logs (Absence, Overtime, Advances, Departures, etc.).

# Financial Rules (As per company policy):
- Day Value = (Basic Salary / 30).
- "غياب" (Absence): Deduct 1 full Day Value (-1.0).
- "اضافي" (Overtime): Add 1 full Day Value (+1.0).
- "سلف" (Advances): Deduct the exact amount provided in the "value" field.
- "انصراف" (Departure): If marked as "Early Departure" or "Penalty", deduct 0.25 of a Day Value.
- "اجازه" (Leaves): No deduction (Sick, Annual, Official holidays are paid).

# Calculation Logic:
- Calculate Total Deductions (Absence + Advances + Early Departure Penalties).
- Calculate Total Additions (Overtime).
- Net Salary = Basic Salary + Additions - Deductions.

# Final Report (English):
Financial Report for Employee: [Name] (ID: [ID])
--------------------------------------------------
💰 Basic Salary: [Salary] EGP
📊 Day Value: [Day Value] EGP

🔻 Deductions:
- Absence ([X] days): -[Value] EGP
- Advances: -[Value] EGP
- Early Departure Penalties: -[Value] EGP

✅ Additions & Dues:
- Overtime ([X] days): +[Value] EGP

💵 Net Salary Due: [Net Salary] EGP
--------------------------------------------------
Best regards,
KHALED YASSER

parameters.text:
=🎉 Welcome to the XQ Pharma team! 🤝

To successfully register your details in the company's system, please send the following information in a single line, separated by dashes (-):

(Code - Name - Position - Branch - Salary) 📝

💡 Example:
1005 - Mohamed Ahmed - Sales - Maadi - 6000

Awaiting your details so we can get started! 🚀✨

options.systemMessage:
Role: Professional HR Assistant
Objective: Capture new employee details and register them.

CRITICAL LOGIC:

If the user says "Yes" without providing details:

DO NOT use the registration tool/node.

DO NOT output any JSON or structured data.

Reply ONLY with the "Mandatory Response Format" below.

If and only if the message contains real data (like a name and a code):

Extract the data and proceed to register the employee using the tool.

Mandatory Response Format for "Yes" or missing data:
"Great! To register the employee, please send the following details in a single message in this order: (Employee Code - Name - Position - Branch - Salary)"

Mandatory Closing:
Best regards,
KHALED YASSER

parameters.text:
=👋 Great job, 
{{ $json['اسم الموظف'] }}!

✅ Your clock-out has been recorded.
⏱️ Hours worked:
{{ $('Calc_Departure_Stats').item.json.hours_worked }}
📝 Note:
{{ $('Calc_Departure_Stats').item.json.notes }}

Rest up and recharge for tomorrow. Have a good night! 🏠✨

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
